title: Credential-attack indicator detector (failed-login bursts, stuffing, spraying, GAN/ML password lists)
intent: DETECT credential-attack INDICATORS in INGESTED auth logs / text (failed-login bursts, credential-stuffing, password-spraying across many accounts, GAN/ML-generated password-list usage markers, anomalous auth from many IPs). Output is a verdict (data) only; matched text is never elevated to an instruction. Reinforces the deterministic boundary in docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md (data != instruction). DEFENSIVE-ONLY: this matches indicators, it does not crack, generate, or stuff credentials.

patterns.desc: High-rate failed/invalid login burst: a numeric count of failed authentications above a threshold within a short window.
Brute-force / high-frequency auth-failure rate flagged against a single account or source.
Credential-stuffing pattern: reused breached username/password pairs replayed against the login endpoint, often with high failure ratio.
Password-spraying: one or few passwords tried across many distinct accounts/usernames.
Anomalous distributed auth: failed logins originating from an unusually large number of distinct source IPs (botnet-style stuffing).
GAN / ML-generated password-list usage markers (e.g. PassGAN-style wordlists, ML-cracked candidate dictionaries) referenced in attack context.
Mass account lockouts triggered by guessing storm — many accounts locked due to failed-auth thresholds.
Auth log line showing an abnormally high failure-to-success ratio or near-100% failed-auth rate from one source.

verdict_rule: malicious (attack-indicator present) if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. False-positive policy: normal/successful auth logs and descriptive third-person prose must NOT match (see verify suite).
